title: choretrum lateriflorum
intro: Choretrum lateriflorum, also known as the New Zealand cherry, is a shrub native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Choretrum lateriflorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and berries, which can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or applied topically as a salve or cream.
